seoTitle: How to Change Your QuickBooks Online Password
metaDescription: Update your QuickBooks Online password to protect sensitive financial and client data, with tips for creating a strong, memorable password.
intro: Since QuickBooks Online holds sensitive financial data, keeping your password current is a simple but important security habit. This guide walks through updating your password and offers a few tips for keeping your account secure, useful for anyone managing business finances, from freelancers to larger finance teams.

steps.heading: Go to your account settings
steps.body: Log in to QuickBooks Online and navigate to the account or security settings area.

steps.heading: Find the password update option
steps.body: Locate the setting that lets you change your current password.

steps.heading: Enter your current and new password
steps.body: Confirm your existing password, then enter a new one that meets QuickBooks' security requirements.

steps.heading: Save the change
steps.body: Confirm the update so your new password takes effect for future logins.

steps.heading: Build a habit of updating it regularly
steps.body: Consider changing your password periodically as part of good account security practices, especially if you manage sensitive client data.

faq.question: How often should I change my QuickBooks Online password?
faq.answer: There's no strict rule, but updating it periodically, and immediately after any suspected security issue, is good practice.

faq.question: What makes a strong QuickBooks Online password?
faq.answer: A mix of length, unique characters, and avoiding reused passwords from other accounts helps keep your login secure.

faq.question: Can I recover my account if I forget my password?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online has a password reset process available from the login screen for account recovery.

faq.question: Does changing my password affect connected apps or integrations?
faq.answer: It generally doesn't disrupt integrations tied to your account, though you should verify any connected apps still function normally after the change.
